title: oxalis stricta subsp. diffusa
intro: Oxalis stricta subsp. diffusa, also known as woodland sorrel or yellow woodsorrel, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, Oxalidaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat digestive issues, as well as its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of oxalis stricta subsp. diffusa are attributed to its high content of vitamins A and C, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and can also be consumed as a salad green or used in soups and sauces.
